message:
fix(dominating): is_connected_dominating_set materialise subgraph before connectedness check (franken_networkx-r8qe)

is_connected_dominating_set passed G.subgraph(S) — a filtered view —
directly into is_connected, which checks via the Rust dispatch and
raises TypeError("expected Graph, DiGraph, MultiGraph, or MultiDiGraph")
because the view isn't a concrete class. Call .copy() on the view so
the induced subgraph becomes a real fnx.Graph before connectedness runs.
This preserves upstream's boolean contract (True on path_graph(5) with
{1,2,3}; False on disconnected graph with {0,2}) on fnx graph inputs.

Gate test_is_connected_dominating_set_accepts_fnx_path_graph compares
against nx.is_connected_dominating_set on both the connected and
disconnected cases.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -12886,7 +12886,10 @@ def is_connected_dominating_set(G, S):
             return False
     if len(S) <= 1:
         return True
-    return is_connected(G.subgraph(S))
+    # is_connected expects a concrete fnx graph class; G.subgraph() returns a
+    # filtered view which the Rust dispatch rejects. Materialise the induced
+    # subgraph via copy() before checking connectedness.
+    return is_connected(G.subgraph(S).copy())
 
 
 def is_kl_connected(G, k, l, low_memory=False):

@@ -471,6 +471,26 @@ def test_connected_dominating_set(self):
         ds = fnx.connected_dominating_set(G)
         assert fnx.is_connected_dominating_set(G, ds)
 
+    def test_is_connected_dominating_set_accepts_fnx_path_graph(self):
+        """Bead franken_networkx-r8qe: must match upstream contract on
+        fnx graph inputs instead of raising AttributeError on G._adj.
+        """
+        import networkx as nx
+
+        fg = fnx.path_graph(5)
+        ng = nx.path_graph(5)
+        assert fnx.is_connected_dominating_set(fg, {1, 2, 3}) == nx.is_connected_dominating_set(
+            ng, {1, 2, 3}
+        )
+        # Disconnected induced subgraph → False on both sides.
+        fg2 = fnx.Graph()
+        fg2.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (2, 3)])
+        ng2 = nx.Graph()
+        ng2.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (2, 3)])
+        assert fnx.is_connected_dominating_set(fg2, {0, 2}) == nx.is_connected_dominating_set(
+            ng2, {0, 2}
+        )
+
     def test_is_kl_connected(self):
         G = fnx.complete_graph(5)
         assert fnx.is_kl_connected(G, 2, 1)
